concepts.title: Biotechnology is the use of biological systems, living organisms, or derivatives thereof, to develop or make products, technologies, and processes.
concepts.description: This is a key definition to remember, as it encompasses a wide range of applications and fields within biotechnology.

concepts.title: Biotechnology has applications in medicine, agriculture, food processing, and environmental conservation.
concepts.description: This is a direct quote from the NCERT textbook and highlights the scope of biotechnology.

concepts.title: Genetic engineering involves the manipulation of genetic material to introduce desirable traits in an organism.
concepts.description: This is a key concept that is frequently asked in board exams, so it's essential to understand the process and its applications.

concepts.title: The 4 Rs of Biotechnology: Recombinant DNA, Replication, Restriction, and Repair.
concepts.description: This mnemonic helps students remember the fundamental steps involved in genetic engineering.

concepts.title: Confusing biotechnology with genetics.
concepts.description: Biotechnology focuses on the application of biological systems to develop products and technologies, whereas genetics deals with the study of heredity and variation.

concepts.title: Biotechnology has led to the development of recombinant DNA technology, gene therapy, and synthetic biology.
concepts.description: These are key units within the field of biotechnology that are often tested in exams.

concepts.title: Biotechnology has numerous applications in medicine, including the production of vaccines, insulin, and other therapeutic proteins.
concepts.description: This is an essential fact to remember, as it highlights the significant impact of biotechnology on human health.

concepts.title: Biotechnology has been instrumental in the development of new crop varieties with desirable traits such as pest resistance and drought tolerance.
concepts.description: This is a key application of biotechnology that is often tested in exams.
